back_translation: On the first day of the week, Mary of Magdala came to the tomb early in the morning while it was still dark, and she saw that the stone had been rolled away from the mouth of the tomb.
So she ran to Simon Peter and the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have placed Him.”
Peter and the other disciple went out to the tomb.
The two ran together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and reached the tomb first.
He stooped down and looked in and saw the linen cloths lying there, but he did not enter inside.
Simon Peter arrived after him and entered the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying there.
The face-cloth that had been on His head was not lying with the linen cloths but was rolled up separately in one place.
Then the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, also entered. He saw and believed.
They still did not understand the Scripture that He must rise from the dead.
So the disciples returned to their homes.
But Mary stood weeping outside the tomb. As she wept, she stooped down and looked into the tomb.
She saw two angels in white clothes sitting there, one at the head, the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had been laid.
The two angels said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping?” She answered, “They have taken my Lord away, and I do not know where they have placed Him.”
When she had said this, she turned back and saw Jesus standing there, but she did not know it was Him.

Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you seeking?” Thinking that He was the gardener, she said to Him, “Sir, if you have taken His body away, please tell me where you have placed Him, so I may take Him.”
Jesus said to her, “Mary.” She turned and said to Him in Hebrew, “Rabboni!” which means Teacher.
Jesus said to her, “Do not cling to Me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. Go and tell My brothers, ‘I am ascending to My Father and your Father, to My God and your God.’”
So Mary of Magdala went and told the disciples, “I have seen the Lord!” and she related what He had spoken to her.

On the evening of that day, which was the first day of the week, while the doors were locked because the disciples were afraid of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst of them, and said to them, “Peace be with you.”
When He had said this, He showed them His hands and His side. The disciples rejoiced when they saw the Lord.
Jesus said to them again, “Peace be with you. As the Father has sent Me, so I send you.”
When He had said this, He breathed His breath upon them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
If you forgive the sins of any, those sins will be forgiven; if you do not forgive any, they will not be forgiven.”
Thomas, also called Didymus, one of the Twelve, was not with them when Jesus came.

So the other disciples told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But Thomas said to them, “Unless I see the marks of the nails in His hands, and put my finger into the marks of the nails, and put my hand into His side, I will absolutely not believe.”
Eight days later, the disciples were inside again, and Thomas was with them. While the doors were locked, Jesus came and stood in the midst and said, “Peace be with you.”
Then He said to Thomas, “Bring your finger here and look at My hands. Bring your hand and put it into My side. Do not be an unbeliever any longer, but be a believer.”
Thomas answered Him, “My Lord and my God!”
Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen Me, you have believed? Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.”
Jesus performed many other signs in the presence of His disciples that are not recorded in this book.
But these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that, believing, you may have life in His name.
